problem_statement: The selected read-only worker path has a runner-registration witness, but the next evidence boundary needs a schema-backed live dispatch endpoint registration witness that defines required endpoint evidence while keeping live runtime authority blocked.
success_criteria: ReadOnlyWorkerRuntimeDispatchEndpointRegistrationWitness validates against a public JSON schema and Foundation Mode example

the witness binds the runner registration witness plus the upstream receipt-store write-path, schema-binding, runtime receipt candidate, runner binding, emitter dry-run, handoff, binding, lease preflight, and rehearsal evidence
live dispatch endpoint registration witness is defined without performing endpoint registration

the future endpoint registration path requires operator approval, live runner registration, endpoint identity digest, route boundary, tenant and actor refs, temporal lease evidence, UAO, Phi_gov, WorkerFailureReceipt, receipt-store evidence, and effect reconciliation
focused tests reject authority drift, contract drift, missing refs, registration evaluation drift, admission drift, receipt-ref drift, count drift, and malformed payloads
non_goals: no launch, production, customer, or support readiness claim
constraints: live dispatch endpoint registration witness is witness-only

future dispatch admission must still require operator approval, live runner registration, endpoint identity digest, endpoint route boundary, temporal lease evidence, UAO, Phi_gov, recovery evidence, runtime emitter evidence, receipt-store evidence, and verification
WorkerFailureReceipt remains mandatory for failed or partial dispatch evidence
raw output retention remains denied and only output digests may be carried
Mfidel atomicity remains preserved and no text-processing decomposition is introduced
evidence_required: runtime dispatch endpoint registration witness validator receipt
focused pytest receipt for dispatch endpoint registration witness behavior
workspace governance preflight receipt before closure claim
